message:
fix: compliance test failures and magic number ratchet (coverage 90%→91%+) (#9745)

* fix: wrap compliance tests in MemoryRouter and fix magic numbers ratchet to restore 91% coverage

Root cause 1: All 12 compliance test files rendered components that
transitively use UnifiedDashboard → DashboardHealthIndicator → useNavigate(),
but tests lacked a Router context. Fixed by:
- Adding MemoryRouter wrapper around each render() call
- Mocking UnifiedDashboard (which also pulls in AddCardModal → useToast)
- Fixing ComplianceFrameworks.test.tsx which imported a named export
  { ComplianceFrameworks } that does not exist — component is default export

Root cause 2: Magic numbers ratchet (no-magic-numbers.test.ts) failed
with 55 violations vs baseline 54. The new kserve_status/useKServeStatus.ts
contained a variable named avgP95 whose suffix '95' was matched by the
reverse-comparison regex as '95 = services.length'. Renamed to avgLatencyMs
to eliminate the false positive without changing any behavior.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: correct mock import paths in CiliumStatus.test.tsx

The test file is nested one extra level deeper in __tests__/ subdirectory,
so '../../../../' is required (not '../../../') to reach src/hooks/ and
src/lib/ from the test file location. The wrong paths caused the mocks
for useCachedCiliumStatus, useGlobalFilters, useDrillDown, and lib/cards
to silently no-op, leaving the component using real implementations that
return empty/default data — causing the 'renders node list' test to fail.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
